attributes.content:
## Overview

The Health Record card gives the workspace a structured home for general health record container linking to lab results, medications, and history.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Health Record acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Document]]** -- Health Record can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Report]]** -- Health Record can feed rollups and summaries that surface trends, status, or outcomes.
- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Health Record can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Health Record.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Health Record can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Contact]]** -- Health Record often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Lab Result]]** -- Lab Result can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Health Record.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Health Record as the structured home for general health record container linking to lab results, medications, and history and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Health Record when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Health Record stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.name: Health Record
cardInfo.summary: General health record container linking to lab results, medications, and history.
